questions.explanation: Real-time refers to predictable timing behavior—meeting deadlines—rather than maximum speed or requiring an RTOS.

options.A: The system must run as fast as possible
options.B: The system must meet timing deadlines deterministically
options.C: The system must be connected to the internet
options.D: The system must use a real-time operating system

questions.question: In an embedded system, what is the most common meaning of "real-time"?

questions.explanation: An AND gate produces a HIGH output only if every input is HIGH.
questions.question: Which digital logic gate outputs HIGH only when all inputs are HIGH?

questions.explanation: git commit records the staged snapshot into the repository history.
questions.question: In Git, which command creates a new commit from staged changes?

questions.explanation: The Nested Vectored Interrupt Controller (NVIC) enables, prioritizes, and dispatches interrupts/exceptions on Cortex-M.
questions.question: On Arm Cortex-M microcontrollers, what is the primary purpose of the NVIC?

questions.explanation: I2C uses SDA/SCL with addressing and open-drain lines, allowing multiple devices on the same bus.
questions.question: You need to connect multiple sensors on the same two-wire bus with device addressing and open-drain signaling. Which interface is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: Static allocation and fixed-size pools reduce fragmentation risk and improve predictability; frequent heap use can degrade long-running systems.

options.A: Use dynamic allocation freely; fragmentation is not an issue on MCUs
options.B: Prefer static allocation and fixed-size pools; minimize new/delete
options.C: Call malloc/free from interrupts to keep latency low
options.D: Rely on exceptions to recover from allocation failures

questions.question: In embedded C++, which approach is generally best to avoid heap fragmentation on long-running microcontroller firmware?

questions.explanation: An integrated debugger provides breakpoints, single-stepping, and variable/memory inspection on the target.
questions.question: In an IDE, which feature most directly allows stepping through code, setting breakpoints, and inspecting variables on a target MCU?

questions.explanation: Half-transfer and transfer-complete interrupts are designed for double-buffer/circular DMA workflows, enabling timely processing with low CPU overhead.

options.A: Poll the DMA transfer counter in a tight loop
options.B: Use DMA half-transfer and transfer-complete interrupts to signal processing of each half
options.C: Disable interrupts during the entire DMA transfer to avoid race conditions
options.D: Trigger processing only from the SysTick timer at a fixed rate

questions.question: A Cortex-M system uses DMA to fill a circular buffer from an ADC. You must process each half-buffer as soon as it is ready with minimal CPU overhead. Which interrupt/event strategy is best?

questions.explanation: Read-only data should be in .rodata and mapped to Flash; declaring it const and mapping sections correctly prevents it from consuming RAM.

options.A: Move the table into the .bss section
options.B: Place the table in a read-only section (e.g., .rodata) mapped to Flash and ensure it is declared const
options.C: Increase the stack size and leave the table in .data
options.D: Enable compiler optimizations so the table is removed automatically

questions.question: Your firmware crashes because a large lookup table is placed in RAM, but it should reside in Flash. Which linker-script change most directly fixes this?

questions.explanation: Yocto/OpenEmbedded uses BitBake recipes to automate and reproduce building toolchains, packages, and complete Linux images.

options.A: Use an RTOS like FreeRTOS and manually copy binaries to an SD card
options.B: Use Yocto/OpenEmbedded with BitBake recipes to generate the image
options.C: Write a Prolog program to infer device tree nodes automatically
options.D: Use only a bare-metal IDE project and a linker script

questions.question: You are building an embedded Linux image and want reproducible builds and automatic generation of a root filesystem, toolchain, and packages from recipes. Which approach best matches this goal?
